message:
feat(store): HLL sparse/dense encoding round-trip + PFDEBUG primitives

crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (+310):

Expands HyperLogLog support in fr-store to cover the encoding
details that PFDEBUG and PFSELFTEST rely on, bringing the sparse
and dense representations to parity with redis's HLL opcode layout.

New free functions / helpers:
 - `as_byte`, `as_str`, `from_byte`: small byte/str adapters for
   the typed HLL encoding enum.
 - `group_read_options`: groups PFDEBUG GETREG options so the
   dispatch table stays readable.
 - `hll_debug_encoding_and_todense_report_dense_representation`:
   verifies that ENCODING and TODENSE both report "dense" after
   a TODENSE promotion.
 - `hll_debug_getreg_returns_full_register_vector`: asserts the
   GETREG subcommand returns all 16384 6-bit registers when the
   HLL is in dense form.
 - `hll_debug_getreg_returns_full_register_vector_and_promotes_sparse_to_dense`:
   GETREG on a sparse HLL triggers promotion to dense and then
   returns the full register vector, matching redis.
 - `hll_debug_sparse_encoding_decode_and_todense_match_redis_contract`:
   decode+todense on a sparse HLL yields the same register set
   as the original encode, pinning the opcode layout.
 - `hll_debug_validate`: validates an HLL byte string structurally
   (magic, version, encoding byte, payload length).
 - `hll_encode`, `hll_estimate`, `hll_parse`, `hll_parse_registers`,
   `hll_rho`, `hll_run_selftest`, `hll_selftest_passes`,
   `hll_sparse_decode`, `hll_sparse_opcodes`, `hll_sparse_should_promote`:
   reusable primitives now called from fr-command PFDEBUG handler.
 - `hll_sparse_decode_matches_redis_opcode_split_limits`: locks
   the XZERO/ZERO/VAL opcode boundaries.
